completeness_boundary: The full cited PDF was reviewed and searched. not_reported means that no explicit statement was found in that source.

probes.question: Does the system query a literature database during idea formulation to assess novelty and identify prior work?
Does a dedicated LLM evaluator select a node that seeds the next experimentation stage?
Can VLM critique change a node's status and be retained for future debugging inside the experiment tree?
Were the completed AI-generated manuscripts assessed through the workshop's blind peer-review process?
Does The AI Scientist-v2 require fixed, human-authored template code as the starting point for experimentation?
Does The AI Scientist-v2 restrict experimentation to a strictly linear sequence in which every refinement builds only on the immediately preceding experiment?
Was the choice of which AI-generated initial ideas to fund and run through the full system made without human selection?
Were all three AI-generated workshop submissions accepted?
Does the source report a probabilistic calibration analysis of the internal LLM evaluator used to select tree nodes?
Does the source report that official workshop peer-review feedback was routed back into the experiment tree during any autonomous run?
Does the source report execution of robotic, wet-lab, or other physical experiments by The AI Scientist-v2?
Does the source report that learned memory is preserved and reused across independent full-pipeline runs?
